title: westringia rigida
intro: Westringia rigida, also known as the 'narrow-leaved rosemary', is a shrubby plant native to Australia, characterized by its small, greenish-blue flowers and needle-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Westringia rigida is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, making it a popular choice among herbalists for its versatility and potential health benefits.
